text.source_text: इतश्च परमेश्वर एव ‘ दहरोऽस्मिन्नन्तराकाशः ’ इत्युच्यते ; यत्कारणमाकाशशब्दः परमेश्वरे प्रसिद्धः — ‘ आकाशो वै नाम नामरूपयोर्निर्वहिता ’ (छा. उ. ८ । १४ । १) ‘ सर्वाणि ह वा इमानि भूतान्याकाशादेव समुत्पद्यन्ते ’ (छा. उ. १ । ९ । १) इत्यादिप्रयोगदर्शनात् । जीवे तु न क्वचिदाकाशशब्दः प्रयुज्यमानो दृश्यते । भूताकाशस्तु सत्यामप्याकाशशब्दप्रसिद्धौ उपमानोपमेयभावाद्यसम्भवान्न ग्रहीतव्य इत्युक्तम् ॥ १७ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: itaśca parameśvara eva ‘ daharo'sminnantarākāśaḥ ’ ityucyate ; yatkāraṇamākāśaśabdaḥ parameśvare prasiddhaḥ — ‘ ākāśo vai nāma nāmarūpayornirvahitā ’ (chā. u. 8 | 14 | 1) ‘ sarvāṇi ha vā imāni bhūtānyākāśādeva samutpadyante ’ (chā. u. 1 | 9 | 1) ityādiprayogadarśanāt | jīve tu na kvacidākāśaśabdaḥ prayujyamāno dṛśyate | bhūtākāśastu satyāmapyākāśaśabdaprasiddhau upamānopameyabhāvādyasambhavānna grahītavya ityuktam || 17 ||

translation.literal_translation: Further, it is the supreme Lord alone that is called 'the small inner space,' because the word akasha is well-established for the supreme Lord, as seen in usages such as 'Akasha is the bearer of name and form' and 'all beings arise from Akasha.' But nowhere do we see akasha used for the individual soul. As for elemental space: even if that word is commonly used there, it cannot be taken here because features like comparison structure and the rest are impossible for that reading, as already explained.
translation.technical_translation: Sutra 1.3.17 bhashya: lexical prasiddhi of akasha in Brahman-sense, plus prior impossibility arguments against bhutakasha, secures dahara as Ishvara.
translation.interpretive_note: Word-usage precedence is made explicit as a supporting pramana.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S03 pass with consistent S04-style inferential framing.
A later philological pass should tighten nuance in long non-dual interpretive paragraphs (1.3.045 especially).
